items.name: Mr. Cheese 🧀 | Funny Yellow Food Character Bundle
items.description:
An adventurous character who feels right at home in cheesy and crispy worlds. 🧀✨

Meet Mr. Cheese, the ultimate funny food character for your next adventure! Whether you are exploring cheesy worlds or just hanging out with friends, this yellow cheese bundle is designed to stand out.

Perfect for roleplay (RP), meme avatars, and fans of cute food characters. This full body character works great in any R15 compatible game.

items.name: ⏳ Young-Hee Head [Squid Game]
items.description:
This is the head of Young-Hee, a giant toy-like doll inspired by a traditional Korean schoolgirl character. She has a round face, smooth plastic skin, and big, wide eyes that seem to follow movement. Her black hair is neatly styled into pigtails with ribbon ties, and she wears a gentle expression. 

Our Store: https://www.roblox.com/catalog?Category=1&CreatorName=FNAF+Realms&CreatorType=Group&salesTypeFilter=1&IncludeNotForSale

✅Favourite for FREE✅

items.name: Montefortino Helmet Type II [PBR]
items.description:
The Montefortino Helmet is the definitive helmet for the Italic region from the 4th century B.C. up to the 1st century A.D. This is the helmet you will see on a roman soldier during the republican period. 

Type 2 Montefortino helmets are dated from 4th - 3rd Century B.C. and are characterised as being taller and with a more bulbous spherical helmet shape, especially compared to the earlier type 1 helmets.

This specific accessory was modeled after the Montefortino find in Apulia (Puglia), Italy and now held in the British Museum. Classifications by John M. Paddock.

Modeled by: OldWizard121

Textured by: OldWizard121
items.searchText:
montefortino helmet type ii [pbr] the montefortino helmet is the definitive helmet for the italic region from the 4th century b.c. up to the 1st century a.d. this is the helmet you will see on a roman soldier during the republican period.

this specific accessory was modeled after the montefortino find in apulia (puglia), italy and now held in the british museum. classifications by john m. paddock.
